senses.definition: plural of sizing; the act or process of determining size or measurements, or the quantities used in determining dimensions or compatibility.
examples: The sizings of the garments were adjusted to fit all customers.
We checked the sizings before placing the order.

senses.definition: the discrimination or prejudice against people based on their size or body shape.
examples: Sizism can occur in the workplace when larger employees are given fewer opportunities.
Raising awareness about sizism helps promote body-inclusive policies.

senses.definition: plural of sizism; instances or forms of size-based discrimination.
examples: The report documents several sizisms observed in hiring.
Addressing sizisms requires policy changes.

senses.definition: believing or implying that size or body type is a measure of worth or capability; biased against people because of their size.
examples: A sizist attitude toward employees is unfair.
They were accused of making sizist comments.

senses.definition: people who hold sizist views; proponents of size-based discrimination.
examples: Some sizists resist anti-discrimination policies.
The conference included panels addressing sizists' arguments.

senses.definition: rare or dialect form related to size; may appear as a nickname or shorthand in some contexts; not widely used in standard English.
examples: The old ledger used the term sizy for a small measurement, but it's obsolete.

senses.definition: a medical term referring to a lack of symmetry, especially in paired organs; also used in embryology to describe asymmetry.
examples: The study examined sizygia in the development of the limbs.
Ssymmetry comparisons revealed sizygia between the specimens.

senses.definition: anatomical term referring to a fused or joined pair of structures; used especially in botany or anatomy.
examples: The sizygium of the plant is where the two petals are fused.

senses.definition: onomatopoeic sound representing a hissing or sizzling noise; used in informal writing or comics.
examples: The sizz of bacon filled the kitchen.
He wrote the sizz in the panel to show heat.

senses.definition: a colloquial or playful term for a person who sizzles; also a character name or brand nickname in some contexts; not a standard word.
examples: The mascot was known as the Sizzard during the game.

senses.definition: present participle of sizzle; making a hissing or sizzling sound or producing heat.
examples: The pan was sizzing as the butter melted.
The weather was sizzing in the noon sun.

senses.definition: a hissing sound of frying or cooking; excitement or energy surrounding something.
examples: The sizzle of bacon filled the kitchen.
The new product has a lot of sizzle in its advertising.

senses.definition: to make a hissing or sizzling sound or to cook with hot oil or fat.
examples: The steak sizzles when it hits the hot pan.
Bacon sizzles in the skillet.

senses.definition: past tense of sizzle; cooked with a hissing sound or frying sound.
examples: The bacon sizzled loudly as it cooked.
The excitement sizzled in the room after the announcement.

senses.definition: something that sizzles, especially a hot dish or event; also a brand name or a person who sizzles in performance.
examples: The steak was a real sizzler.
The concert labeled as a sizzler drew a large crowd.

senses.definition: plural of sizzler; items or events that sizzle; multiple hot dishes or performances.
examples: The cafe offered several sizzlers on the menu.
The festival included many sizzlers that drew crowds.

senses.definition: the sound or act of sizzling; also, a quick, exciting moment.
examples: The sizzles from the skillet filled the kitchen.
The movie has a lot of sizzles and bursts of energy.

senses.definition: third-person singular present of sizzle; makes a hissing sound or cooks with high heat.
examples: The bacon sizzles as it cooks.
The advertisements sizzle with bright colors.

senses.definition: very hot or exciting; producing a sizzling sound; lively and energetic.
examples: The sizzling steak smelled wonderful.
The concert was a sizzling performance.

senses.definition: present participle of sizzle; actively producing a sizzling sound or heat.
examples: The pan was sizzling on the stove.
Sizzling rays warmed the beach.

senses.definition: in a very exciting or hotly energetic way; with sizzling energy.
examples: The performance was sizzlingly good.
The debate heated up sizzlingly as the crowd watched.

senses.definition: plural of sizzling event or sound; brief moments of excitement.
examples: The show had several sizzlings that got the crowd shouting.
Sizzlings of activity marked the festival days.

senses.definition: a given name or surname (Dutch diminutive) meaning 'Jack' or used as a nickname; may refer to a person named Sjaak.
examples: Sjaak van der Meer gave a talk on the project.

senses.definition: a thick, heavy stick used as a weapon, traditionally in southern Africa; a whip.
examples: The guard carried a sjambok as a deterrent.
Historically, sjamboks were used by livestock herders.

senses.definition: past tense of sjambok; to strike with a sjambok.
examples: The guard sjamboked the escapee with a heavy blow.
The animal was sjamboked to subdue it.

senses.definition: present participle of sjambok; the act of striking with a sjambok.
examples: The officer was sjamboking the suspect during the chase.

senses.definition: past tense of sjambok; struck with a sjambok.
examples: The prisoner was sjambokked after the escape attempt.

senses.definition: present participle of sjambok; the act of using a sjambok to strike.
examples: The officer was sjambokking the detainee to restrain him.
